summary.text: Technical leader and software engineer with 8+ years architecting and delivering distributed backend systems, real-time data platforms, and client-server applications across government and enterprise environments. Background includes distributed Java/Spring microservices for a legacy-to-modern banking platform migration serving 5M+ users. Leads cross-functional engineering teams, with a track record of driving automation and AI-agent tooling (LangChain, CrewAI, RAG) to increase engineering efficiency. Patent holder in predictive analytics, with an M.Sc. in Digital Transformation and Innovation and a B.Tech in Software Engineering.

stats.label: users served on migrated banking platform

bullets: Architected and implemented a high-performance distributed backend service for production AI inference by compressing a 15GB model to 5GB and deploying it through containerized REST APIs, reducing latency 12× (3,600ms → 300ms) while supporting 100,000+ requests/day.

Architected and led end-to-end delivery of an LLM-based summarization service for a 20-person team, cutting response time on 1,000–2,000 daily citizen comments from 1 day to 4 hours (75% reduction), with built-in auto-redaction for AI safety compliance.

Designed and standardized scalable distributed data-processing architecture using Databricks, Apache Spark, Airflow, and MLflow, establishing production engineering patterns adopted across multiple engineering teams.

Designed and shipped two executive-facing R Shiny/Plotly dashboards independently, replacing a multi-day cross-team manual process and analyzing survey data across 100,000+ respondents — both adopted at the executive level.
experience.impact: Accelerated production AI inference by 12× (3,600ms to 300ms) while supporting 100,000+ daily requests by compressing and deploying a 15GB model as containerized REST APIs.

bullets: Architected and delivered a real-time distributed monitoring platform supporting national electricity monitoring (7,200+ rows/day/province, 1M+ historical rows), adopted by major energy providers and the public.

Designed and implemented a distributed data ingestion platform integrating 12+ data sources, reducing refresh latency from 24 hours to 5 minutes while processing over 1 million production records daily.
Led a team of 5 engineers and served as primary client contact coordinating 2 additional teams — owning requirements gathering, KPI definition, and on-time delivery end-to-end.
Diagnosed root-cause issues and redesigned the search architecture (algorithm + NLP pipeline), lifting accuracy from 35% to 65%, cutting complaints 20%, and growing traffic 10%.
experience.impact: Reduced data-refresh latency from 24 hours to 5 minutes while processing 1M+ daily records by building a distributed ingestion platform that unified 12+ data sources.

bullets: Redesigned the university library's client-server booking system (PHP/MySQL) to support 50–60 daily users and up to 30 requests per 3-hour window, adapting the system under tight deadlines for pandemic-driven demand.
Delivered booking and resource-access features end-to-end within an Agile team, from requirements through deployment.
experience.impact: Enabled reliable access for 50–60 daily users and up to 30 booking requests per three-hour window by redesigning the university library's PHP/MySQL booking system under pandemic-driven demand.

bullets: Built and maintained distributed Java Spring microservices supporting a legacy-to-modern client-server migration for a banking platform serving 5M+ users.
Developed Angular front-end interfaces and integrated CI/CD into the team's workflow, partnering with the Scrum Master to deliver user stories on sprint timelines.
experience.impact: Supported a legacy-to-modern banking migration serving 5M+ users by building Java/Spring microservices and integrating Angular interfaces with the team's CI/CD delivery workflow.
